@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In the contributing calls, the agent naively inferred "empirical" by searching for the literal word in the full text and extracted the publication year as the first 4‑digit number in the document (see execute_python code and initial Mongo filter), causing it to miss valid empirical papers published after 2016 and return an empty result.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The execute_python step incorrectly extracted publication years using re.findall(r'(19|20)\d{2}', text), which due to the capturing group returned only '19' or '20' instead of full years (e.g., 2018), causing pub_year to remain None for all documents and leading the agent to return no matches despite valid data from the Mongo and SQLite queries.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent’s execute_python steps filtered papers by the literal substring “empirical” in the full text and extracted the publication year as the minimum 20xx found (rather than the actual paper year), resulting in an empty set ([]) despite relevant documents, so it prematurely concluded no matches existed.

B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent filtered documents by simply matching the word "empirical" anywhere in the full text and heuristically extracted the year by finding any 20xx in the text (execute_python after querying paper_docs with a text regex), so its plan never actually identified papers with an 'empirical' contribution or reliable publication years, leading to an incorrect set and counts.
B. Failure modes encountered:
